Crystal_Structure_Reference: Horiuchi, S.; Kagawa, F.; Hatahara, K.; Kobayashi, K.; Kumai, R.; Murakami, Y.; Tokura, Y., Nature Communications (2012), 3, 1308. DOI: 10.1038/ncomms2322
Evidence: Experimental crystal structure determined by synchrotron single-crystal X-ray diffraction and deposited in the Cambridge Structural Database (CCDC 909441; CSD REFCODE KOWYEA02). The crystal crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric orthorhombic space group Pn (No. 7), which is crystallographically compatible with piezoelectricity. The γ-polymorph exhibits ferroelectricity arising from cooperative proton transfer (prototropy), as first reported by Horiuchi et al. (Nature Communications, 2012). The piezoelectric properties were experimentally measured by Horiuchi et al. (Journal of Materials Chemistry C, 2018) using the Berlincourt method (direct piezoelectric effect). The experimentally measured longitudinal piezoelectric coefficient is d33 = 7.5 ± 0.4 pC/N. The crystal consists of discrete neutral 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole molecules linked through intermolecular hydrogen bonds, confirming its molecular crystal nature.
Research_Paper_Name: Piezoelectricity of Strongly Polarized Ferroelectrics in Prototropic Organic Crystals
Notes: Experimental crystal structure obtained from the Cambridge Structural Database.
Crystal structure deposited by S. Horiuchi, F. Kagawa, K. Hatahara, K. Kobayashi, R. Kumai, Y. Murakami and Y. Tokura.
The deposited structure corresponds to the ferroelectric γ-polymorph used in the subsequent piezoelectric investigations.
Piezoelectricity was experimentally confirmed in Journal of Materials Chemistry C (2018) using the Berlincourt method.
Reported direct longitudinal piezoelectric coefficient: d33 = 7.5 ± 0.4 pC/N.
The crystal is a single-component organic molecular crystal containing neutral 2-methyl-1H-benzimidazole molecules.
The crystal crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric space group Pn (No. 7), satisfying the crystallographic requirement for piezoelectricity.
This crystal is an experimentally verified ferroelectric and piezoelectric molecular crystal suitable for inclusion in the molecular piezoelectric crystal database.
